mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output, which boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 12-month rolling rank IC of INDPRO change vs 20-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.03 or negative for any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period signals weakening labour demand, reducing consumer income and spending, which depresses corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When 12-month CPI inflation exceeds the federal funds rate, the resulting negative real interest rate encourages excessive risk-taking but also signals impending monetary tightening, causing S&P 500 to decline over a 60-day horizon as markets price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in the majority of episodes where 12m CPI change > FEDFUNDS, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,800 thousand units combined with a federal funds rate below 3% signal robust demand without imminent monetary tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as construction and related sectors thrive.
failure_condition: In >60% of instances where HOUST > 1800 and FEDFUNDS < 3, SPX 20-day returns are not positive, or the average excess return over baseline is zero.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly gains in nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labour market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward SPX returns is negative or below 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPI exceeding 0.3% signals rising inflation pressure, which prompts expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, thereby reducing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in >50% of months where MoM CPI > 0.3%, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year growth in retail sales outpaces GDP growth, it suggests consumption-led expansion that is unsustainable and leads to inventory overhang, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as the economy rebalances.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > GDPC1 YoY growth
failure_condition: In >50% of quarters where RSAFS YoY growth > GDPC1 YoY growth, SPX 60-day returns are positive, or the hypothesis fails to produce a rank IC above 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2%, the monetary environment is highly accommodative, and further declines in the rate compress discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums narrow.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2%, average 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly greater than zero, or negative returns occur in more than 45% of such episodes.
